title: SEAM Route Fragment (x-seam-schema v1)
description: Concrete JSON Schema for a SEAM route fragment (x-seam-schema: v1). A fragment is a per-service OpenAPI 3.1 paths/components fragment plus SEAM extension fields. Authored alongside its companion design note docs/notes/route-fragment-schema.md, which is the authority on placement decisions and on the cross-field constraints that this schema cannot express and the Go validator (seam lint + runtime quarantine) must enforce on top. References docs/plan/plan.md Data Models, Architecture, Open Questions and ADR-001.
$comment: additionalProperties is deliberately true at every level: OpenAPI-native keys are validated for OpenAPI correctness by pb33f libopenapi (not by this schema), and unknown x-* keys pass through unchecked so a v1 validator does not reject a fragment carrying a forward field (x-requires-approval, or a v2 marker). x-seam-schema is the detectable escape hatch if the format itself evolves. Only the SEAM-owned extension keys named below are shape-validated here.

openapi.$comment: Optional. A fragment is a paths/components fragment, not a standalone OpenAPI document; the merge layer may synthesize or strip this.

info.$comment: Optional OpenAPI info; informational for a fragment.

paths.$comment: OpenAPI requires path template keys to begin with '/'. Every path item is validated as a SEAM path item.

components.$comment: Optional reusable OpenAPI components (schemas, parameters, ...). Not SEAM-validated beyond being an object; libopenapi owns OpenAPI validity.

x-seam-schema.$comment: Fragment-root only. Versions the fragment FORMAT SEAM parses; distinct from x-api-version which versions the contract SEAM serves. const so a v2 fragment is rejected (not shape-guessed) by a v1 validator.

x-seam-owner.$comment: Fragment-root only; required day one. Redundant in-file declaration of the owning service, checked against the mounted parent directory at runtime (validator-side: must equal the directory the fragment was loaded from).

x-api-version.$comment: Fragment-root only. The literal _unversioned is rejected here (the pattern requires v then a non-zero digit); it is SEAM-assigned, never authored.

x-upstream.$comment: Fragment-root. The single upstream base URL this fragment forwards to. Mutually exclusive with x-upstream-map. Host membership in the operator-owned allowlist is validator-side (manifest-level, not fragment-level).

x-upstream-map.$comment: Fragment-root. Multi-instance map: designated instance parameter (x-instance-param) -> per-instance entry. Mutually exclusive with x-upstream; requires x-instance-param.

x-instance-param.$comment: Fragment-root. Required with x-upstream-map, forbidden without it. Must name a path parameter present in EVERY path the fragment declares (validator-side, cross-path).

x-upstream-strip-prefix.$comment: Fragment-root shorthand rewrite: a literal prefix removed from every matched template. Must be a prefix of every declared path (validator-side, cross-path). Upstream-facing: forbidden on an x-adapter fragment.

x-upstream-plaintext.$comment: Fragment-root. Required when x-upstream is http://. Mutually exclusive with x-upstream-map (per-entry plaintext is used there instead).

x-upstream-tls.$comment: Fragment-root upstream transport policy; absent means verify against system trust store with hostname checking. Upstream-facing: taken from targetVersion on an x-adapter fragment.

x-vault-path.$comment: Fragment-root. Which OpenBao secret to fetch. Paired with x-inject-as (both or neither). Co-ownership with x-seam-owner (prefix seam/routes/<owner>/*) and allowlist membership are validator-side.

x-inject-as.$comment: Fragment-root. How to inject the secret. Paired with x-vault-path (both or neither).

x-credential-probe.$comment: Fragment-root. A designated safe request + per-fragment probe interval. Forbidden on a pass-through fragment (one declaring neither x-vault-path nor x-inject-as).

x-breaker.$comment: Fragment-root tuning of the per-upstream circuit breaker. On by default (infrastructure guard); enabled:false is the opt-out. Upstream-facing: forbidden on an x-adapter fragment.

x-required-scope.$comment: Optional fragment-root DEFAULT; any operation-level x-required-scope REPLACES it (validator-side), never merges. Conjunctive array; bare string sugar for one element.

x-fanout-scope.$comment: Fragment-root. Gates the _all fan-out in addition to the operation-level scope. Parsed and carried from Phase 10; enforced from Phase 7.

x-seam-deprecated.$comment: Fragment-root object marking every route deprecated. Feeds Deprecation/Sunset headers and the retirement metric.

x-adapter.$comment: Fragment-root. Declarative request/response transforms delegating to a live targetVersion. Mutually exclusive with x-upstream/x-upstream-map and every upstream-facing field.

x-unscrubbable.$comment: Allowed at fragment-root OR operation level. Acknowledgement that a route runs unscrubbed (response cannot be scanned for echoed secrets).

x-requires-approval.$comment: RESERVED forward-compat field (docs/notes/approval-gated-routes.md, accepted but unphased). Not yet enforced; declared here so the first real fragment can carry it without a schema change.

ownerToken.$comment: One flat segment, no slashes, no dots — simultaneously a legal directory name, ConfigMap suffix, filename stem and OpenBao path segment.

apiVersion.$comment: v<positive integer>, ordered numerically. No dotted/dated/v1beta1 forms; _unversioned is SEAM-assigned and rejected as an author value.

duration.$comment: Closed duration grammar shared by x-quota.window and x-loop-guard.window. No calendar-aligned form (no mo/y). Tumbling window anchored at process start; restart resets it. A value above 168h is a lint WARNING, not a schema failure (validator-side).

scopeString.$comment: Colon-delimited service:action (e.g. k8s-ro:get, argocd:sync) including three-segment control-plane scopes (seam:ops:read, seam:scopes:read-all). At least two segments.

scopeArray.description: Conjunctive set of scopes a caller must hold. A bare string is sugar for a one-element array.
scopeArray.$comment: Duplicates collapse and order is insignificant (validator-side normalization); uniqueItems flags obvious dups.

acknowledged.$comment: The only accepted value for fields that exist to make an exposure enumerable (insecureSkipVerify, x-upstream-plaintext, x-unscrubbable). No false form — absence is refusal, not permission.

not.description: IP-literal hosts are rejected outright: they defeat the enumeration the upstream-host allowlist exists to provide.

upstreamUrl.$comment: Absolute http(s) URL; https is preferred (a live credential is injected into every forwarded request). Host membership in the operator-owned allowlist is validator-side.

vaultPath.$comment: Must resolve inside the OpenBao allowlist prefix seam/routes/* and be co-owned: seam/routes/<x-seam-owner>/*. The prefix shape is encoded; equality with the fragment's owner is validator-side.

injectAs.$comment: kind:header|query -> name required; kind:bearer -> name omitted/rejected (implies Authorization: Bearer <secret>).

caBundle.$comment: Names a PEM key in the seam-upstream-ca ConfigMap, resolved as a file under /etc/gateway/upstream-ca/.

anyOf.$comment: no trailing slash — applied to all paths, so a trailing / would strip the leading / off the remainder
no templated segments — a strip is a literal, not a rewrite template

upstreamStripPrefix.$comment: Fragment-root literal path prefix (leading /, minLength 2 so not the bare '/', no trailing /, no parameters) removed from every matched template after the designated instance segment is dropped — the ergonomic form of step 3's upstream-path rewrite. Must be a prefix of EVERY path the fragment declares (validator-side, cross-path: a partly-applicable strip silently forwards one fragment's routes to two different upstream path shapes). Upstream-facing: forbidden on an x-adapter fragment.

enabled.$comment: Default true; false is the lint-flagged, /config/status-enumerated opt-out.

breaker.$comment: All optional; absent means the SEAM default policy owned by Passive Route Health.

maxRepeats.$comment: Count of identical (normalized-hash) requests tolerated within one window before the guard fires.

unit.$comment: Lowercase label: usd, credits, tokens, requests. SEAM never converts between units.
MUST be byte-identical to the same route's x-cost-per-call.unit (validator-side equality check).

path.$comment: A designated safe upstream path. Must be a route the fragment serves (validator-side, cross-path).

interval.$comment: Per-fragment default; an x-upstream-map entry may override per instance.

plaintext.$comment: Required when this entry's url is http:// (plaintext is per-entry, never inherited). Mutually exclusive with inheriting a fragment-level marker — there is none on a map fragment.

requiredScope.$comment: Per-instance authorization: scope(s) a caller must additionally hold to reach THIS instance. Union with operation-level x-required-scope at stage 5 (validator/runtime).

upstreamMapEntry.$comment: vaultPath/injectAs bind per-instance (both or neither), defaulting to the fragment-level fields when omitted. The effective pair must resolve both-or-neither per instance (validator-side merge).

upstreamMap.$comment: Keys are instance names selected by the x-instance-param segment. Reserved keys: _all (fan-out) and _default (single-instance reporting key).

since.$comment: ISO date the deprecation was declared; populates Deprecation directly.

sunset.$comment: Optional advisory removal date; emitted as Sunset verbatim.

brownout.$comment: Ordered, non-overlapping RFC 3339 windows, each inside [since, sunset]; requires sunset. Machine-written by seam-retirement-evaluator. Ordering/non-overlap/range and the sunset requirement are enforced below + validator-side.

seamDeprecated.$comment: A bare x-seam-deprecated: true is a lint error — it fails type:object here.

targetVersion.$comment: Names a LIVE served x-api-version this fragment adapts into (validator-side: must exist after merge).

adapter.$comment: Each list is an ordered sequence of transforms from a CLOSED vocabulary owned by Version Migration Strategy §2 / Phase 8. The transform object shape is intentionally permissive here (items: object) and will be tightened when §2 finalizes the vocabulary; no expressions, no scripting.

x-upstream-path-template.$comment: Path-item-level authoritative rewrite in the UPSTREAM's own terms. Must name no designated instance parameter and every {param} must exist in the matched template (validator-side, cross-field with x-instance-param and the sibling path template). Upstream-facing: forbidden on an x-adapter fragment.

x-required-scope.$comment: Operation-level authority; REPLACES any fragment-root default (validator-side), never merges.

constraint-quota-requires-cost.$comment: A budget with no per-call draw can never be decremented, so it caps nothing while reading as though it does.

constraint-entry-vault-inject-paired.$comment: Placeholder kept intentionally inert; the effective per-instance both-or-neither resolution requires merging fragment-level defaults with the entry and is enforced by the validator (cannot be expressed per-entry in isolation).

constraint-adapter-excludes-upstream-fie.$comment: An adapting fragment delegates to targetVersion's route; every upstream-facing field is taken from the target. (A path-item x-upstream-path-template on an adapter fragment is likewise illegal — enforced validator-side, cross-level.)

constraint-upstream-xor-map.$comment: A fragment declares exactly one upstream target by construction; the reverse direction is covered by constraint-forwarding-fragment-has-upstream.

constraint-forwarding-fragment-has-upstr.$comment: A non-adapter fragment must declare an upstream target (x-upstream or x-upstream-map). An x-adapter fragment declares neither (excluded above).

constraint-instance-param-only-with-map.$comment: x-instance-param without x-upstream-map is a lint error.

constraint-upstream-excludes-instance-pa.$comment: A single-instance route has no instance-selector segment.

constraint-vault-inject-paired.$comment: Both or neither. A fragment declaring neither is a legal pass-through proxy route. On an x-adapter fragment both are already forbidden, so this is consistent.

constraint-passthrough-has-no-probe.$comment: There is no credential to validate against the upstream on a pass-through route.

constraint-plaintext-required-on-http.$comment: Plaintext acknowledgement is required for any hop that does not leave an authenticated network path.

constraint-plaintext-excludes-map.$comment: On a map fragment acknowledgement is per-entry plaintext, never fragment-wide.
